li.p: To find the y-intercept, compute K(0)={\left(0+1\right)\mathopen{}\left(0+8\right)}=8\text{.} So the y-intercept is {\left(0,8\right)}\text{.}
The quadratic function is given in factored form. We can see that subbing in x=-1 or x=-8 would result in the output being 0\text{.} So the x-intercepts are (-1, 0) and (-8, 0)\text{.}
